results.title: Conrad Ramirez
highlights:
Execution-oriented healthcare technology and operations executive with enterprise responsibility for analytics, AI, EMR platforms, and acute-care operational domains at Universal Health Services. I lead at the intersection of clinical workflows, operational performance, data, and AI, combining strategic leadership with direct execution inside the systems that support care delivery. Serve as executive lead for enterprise analytics and AI initiatives, working extensively with Palantir to model and operationalize clinical and operational data. Lead vendor evaluation, model comparison, and platform selection, while also designing and deploying custom AI agents integrated directly into clinician workflows through background automation—delivering insight without adding burden. Establish clinical safety, regulatory alignment, explainability, governance, and value-realization frameworks for AI deployments. These initiatives have driven productivity gains, reduced reliance on contract labor, shifted staff toward higher-complexity work, and delivered sustained, measurable financial impact. Provide enterprise oversight of Cerner EMR strategy, deployment, and optimization across Acute, Behavioral Health, and Ambulatory settings. Lead large-scale Behavioral Health go-lives and implemented a centralized intake model to improve access and throughput. Maintain executive accountability for HIM, Coding, and CDI across Acute Care, including centralizing coding teams and establishing a standardized Release of Information (ROI) governance model generating $1M+ in annual revenue.
...

If you or someone in your network was impacted and is looking to apply deep healthcare experience inside a health system, I encourage you to explore opportunities here:
...
AI in Action | As guest speaker at HLTH Inc. 2025 in Vegas this week, UHS SVP Mike Nelson stated: "While the healthcare industry debates AI’s ROI, UHS has already delivered tens of millions in net new revenue, improved staff productivity and accelerated denials recovery by strategically applying AI. Using SmarterDx’s clinical-AI platform, UHS runs an automated second-pass review of every patient chart—analyzing over 30,000 data points—to close documentation gaps, capture missed diagnoses, and generate AI-powered appeals in minutes, all while reducing clinician burden. Now under the Smarter Technologies umbrella, the expanded platform brings agentic AI and workflow automation to administrative and financial operations, enabling smarter, faster and more accurate revenue cycle management across one of the nation’s largest healthcare organizations."

results.title: Universal Health Services AI Adoption Tracker | Larridin | AI Initiative Tracker
highlights:
Universal Health Services (UHS) has positioned itself as a leader in healthcare AI transformation through strategic partnerships and targeted technology deployments[1]. The company has partnered with Hippocratic AI to deploy generative AI agents for post-discharge patient follow-up calls, initially launching at Summerlin Hospital Medical Center in Las Vegas and Texoma Medical Center in Texas[1]. These AI agents have achieved patient satisfaction ratings of 9 out of 10, leading to expansion across additional UHS facilities[2]. CEO Marc Miller has stated that UHS has been 'exploring and experimenting with AI for several years now' and that the health system is committed to responsibly determining 'how AI can effectively support the patient care experience'[1]. UHS has also conducted pilots with AI-driven behavioral health solutions, including voice analysis technology through partnerships with companies like Eleos Health[3].

results.title: Benjamin Deck
highlights:
Healthcare AI Leader | Building Enterprise AI Capabilities from Strategy to Scale
...
Assistant Director of AI and Data Science at [UHS](https://www.linkedin.com/company/uhs)
...
I lead AI strategy and data science at Universal Health Services, where I built the enterprise data science function from the ground up — from first hire to a team serving 27 acute care facilities and 100,000+ employees. My work sits at the intersection of AI, healthcare operations, and organizational strategy. I've learned that the biggest barriers to AI adoption in health systems aren't technical — they're strategic. Getting a model to work is the easy part. Getting legal, clinical, IT, and executive stakeholders aligned on how to deploy it responsibly at scale is the challenge. Before UHS, I completed a PhD in Applied Cognitive and Brain Sciences at Drexel University and spent three years coordinating clinical research at the University of Pennsylvania. My background gives me a deep respect for clinical evidence, rigorous methodology, and the complexity of the systems we're trying to improve. I'm increasingly focused on how health systems can move beyond AI experimentation toward enterprise-scale transformation — and on the strategic and organizational challenges that make that transition so difficult.
...

results.title: Gignesh S
highlights:
Data Scientist | ML/DL • NLP • Big Data • Cloud (AWS | Azure | GCP) | Driving Predictive Analytics & Real-Time Insights in Healthcare & Finance | Enabling Business Growth with AI
...
Data Scientist at [UHS](https://www.linkedin.com/company/uhs)
...
Data Scientist with 4+ years of experience helping businesses in healthcare and finance turn data into smarter decisions. Expertise spans predictive analytics, NLP, computer vision, and deep learning, with a focus on delivering measurable outcomes. Achievements include reducing churn by 18% through predictive modeling, building fraud detection systems with 85%+ precision, designing ETL pipelines that cut data preparation time by 40%, and developing recommendation systems that increased cross-sell revenue by 20%. Skilled in leveraging cloud and big data platforms such as AWS, Azure, GCP, Spark, and Snowflake, while deploying scalable ML solutions using MLOps best practices. Beyond model development, strong focus on creating dashboards and insights that empower real-time decision-making. Passionate about applying AI to solve real-world challenges and eager to connect with professionals and teams driving the future of data-driven innovation.
...
### Data Scientist at [UHS](https://www.linkedin.com/company/uhs) (Current)
...
Designed and deployed predictive models using Python (scikit-learn, XGBoost, CatBoost) on 10M+ records, enabling early identification of at-risk customers and reducing churn by 18%, improving revenue retention. Built and fine-tuned deep learning pipelines with TensorFlow, PyTorch, and Keras for medical image classification and NLP sentiment analysis, reaching 92%+ accuracy with HIPAA-compliant data handling and PHI anonymization. Developed ETL pipelines with PySpark, Airflow, and Kafka to ingest and transform diverse sources (APIs, flat files, cloud warehouses), cutting preprocessing time by 40% and accelerating insights. Created cloud-native ML deployment frameworks on AWS SageMaker, Docker, and Kubernetes with CI/CD (Jenkins), reducing deployment lead time by 50% and improving rollout reliability. Designed Tableau and Power BI dashboards with dynamic KPIs, drill-downs, and DAX measures, enabling real-time tracking of patient outcomes, financial KPIs, and operations, improving decision-making efficiency by 30%. Conducted A/B testing and multivariate experiments, applying hypothesis testing and Bayesian inference, which drove a 12% lift in user conversion rates and optimized feature prioritization. Optimized large-scale SQL and Snowflake queries with indexing, partitioning, and caching, cutting runtime by 25% and boosting analyst productivity. Designed a real-time fraud detection system using anomaly detection, clustering, and ensemble ML algorithms, identifying fraudulent claims with 85% precision and preventing millions in revenue leakage. Collaborated with Agile teams to align technical solutions with business goals, ensuring compliance with HIPAA, GDPR, and SOX.

As a Lead AI/ML Engineer on our Behavioral Health Technology team, you will design, develop, and implement enterprise-scale machine learning systems and artificial intelligence solutions. Our team’s mission is to advance clinical and administrative excellence in behavioral health by leveraging advanced technologies like deep learning, natural language processing, and generative AI. In this role, you will have the unique opportunity to work on highly impactful datasets, architect production-ready ML pipelines, and collaborate with cross-functional partners to build cutting-edge solutions that directly improve health outcomes. You will leverage modern AI/ML frameworks, cloud environments (such as Azure, AWS, or GCP), and automated MLOps workflows to deliver scalable, secure, and responsible AI applications.
...
- Own Discovery, technical scoping, system Design, build, and deploy production-grade machine learning models and scalable AI solutions to address complex business challenges, emphasizing responsible and ethical use of AI
...
- Design and implement end-to-end MLOps pipelines, including data ingestion, feature engineering, model training, validation, deployment, and automated monitoring
...
- Evaluate emerging AI/ML trends, frameworks, and methodologies to inform solution design, drive strategic innovation, and continuously optimize existing models
...
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including data scientists, software engineers, and product managers, to translate complex business requirements into robust, reusable AI capabilities
...
- Troubleshoot, optimize, and maintain deployed models and pipelines to ensure high availability, scalability, and performance in production environments
...
- Use enterprise-approved AI tools to streamline workflows, automate tasks, and drive continuous improvement within the engineering lifecycle
...
### Required Qualifications
...
- Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related quantitative field (or equivalent global degree)
- 10+ years of experience in Software Development industry
- 2+ years of AI/ML engineering experience, with a proven track record of designing, building, and deploying ML models in a production environment
- 2+ years of experience with Python and core ML libraries
- 2+ years of experience building and automating end-to-end MLOps pipelines (e.g., using MLflow, Kubeflow, Airflow, or cloud-native tooling)
- Experience with cloud platform environments (e.g., Azure, AWS, or Google Cloud Platform) and containerization technologies (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes)
...
### Preferred Qualifications
...
- Experience with Generative AI, Large Language Models (LLMs), prompt engineering, or retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) frameworks
- Experience with distributed computing frameworks (e.g., Spark, Ray) and high-performance computing
- Experience working in the healthcare sector, specifically in clinical or behavioral health technology domains
- Solid understanding of data engineering principles, relational and non-relational databases (SQL, NoSQL, Vector databases)
- Proven excellent communication and presentation skills, with the ability to explain complex technical AI/ML concepts to non-technical stakeholders

results.title: Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
highlights:
Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
...
# Data Scientist
...
Optum is seeking a Data Science professional who is passionate about applying machine learning, Generative AI, and Large Language Models (LLMs) to solve complex healthcare problems on a scale. Imagine working with massive volumes of healthcare data spanning clinical, claims, behavioral, and digital interactions – powering intelligent personalization, discovery, and decision support for millions of members, providers, and care teams.
...
At Optum, the Data Science mission is to transform healthcare data into robust, scalable AI solutions, including LLM based and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) systems that combine enterprise knowledge with advanced language models to deliver accurate, contextual, and trustworthy insights. These solutions help uncover deep usage patterns, member journeys, and care signals that inform and continuously evolve Optum’s digital products, clinical platforms, and healthcare services.
...
Your work will directly influence how healthcare consumers engage with digital experiences, how clinicians access timely insights, and how business and care strategies are shaped through data. By joining Optum’s data science team, you will be part of an exciting journey to build advanced ML and GenAI driven products, grounded in responsible AI principles, that operate at enterprise scale and create meaningful impact across the healthcare ecosystem.
...
- Design, develop, and deploy advanced analytics, machine learning, and Generative AI solutions to address complex healthcare and business problems using largescale structured and unstructured data
...
- Applies advanced techniques including statistics, machine learning, simulation, and mathematical modelling
...
- Build and optimize LLM based applications, including Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) frameworks, to enable accurate, context aware, and explainable insights from enterprise healthcare data sources such as clinical notes, claims, provider data, and policy documents
...
- Develop, evaluate, and finetune machine learning and GenAI models, including prompt engineering, retrieval strategies, and model evaluation techniques to improve accuracy, relevance, and performance
...
- Translate complex analytical and GenAI outputs into clear, actionable insights for business stakeholders, product teams, and leadership to support data driven decision making
...
- Support prototyping, validation, and deployment of GenAI and ML solutions, working with engineering teams to ensure solutions are scalable, secure, and maintainable. Continuously stay current with advancements in LLMs, GenAI frameworks, and healthcare AI trends, contributing innovative ideas and improvements to Optum’s capabilities
...
### Required Qualifications:
...
- 4+ years of experience in handling/active development in data science projects
- 4+ years of experience in Microsoft Azure Cloud, Databricks, Mlflow, Spark SQL & Python
...
- Experience using Machine Learning Algorithms: Linear & Logistic Regression, Time Series analysis, Support Vector Machine, K-Means clustering, K-Nearest Neighbors Decision Trees, Random Forest, Naive Bayes, PCA, SVD, Artificial Neural Networks, Association Rules, Genetic Algorithm, Optimization, Bagging and Boosting algorithms etc.
...
- Understanding of data, schema, data model, machine learning and how to bring efficiency in big data related life cycle
...

Optum, part of UnitedHealth Group, is a global health services and technology company revolutionizing healthcare delivery through data analytics, pharmacy benefits, and digital health solutions that connect millions to better care. Headquartered in Eden Prairie, Minnesota, Optum serves patients, providers, payers, and pharmacies worldwide with innovative platforms for care optimization, claims processing, and population health management.

results.title: [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II | Wfh
highlights:
# [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II
...
Note: The job is a remote job and is open to candidates in USA. Cotiviti is a healthcare technology company focused on delivering better care at lower costs through advanced technology and data analytics. The Senior Generative AI Scientist II will work with a team to solve healthcare issues using machine learning and AI techniques, delivering value through innovative data solutions. Responsibilities • Work with key stakeholders within Research and Development as well as Business Operations, along with Product Management to assess the potential value and risks associated with business problems that have the potential to be solved using machine learning and Artificial Intelligence techniques • Develop an exploratory data analysis approach to verify the initial hypothesis associated with potential Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning use cases • Document your approach, thinking and results in standard approaches to allow other data scientists to collaborate with you on this work • Prepare your final trained model and develop a validation test set for QA • Work with ML Ops/Production operations to deploy your model into production and support them in monitoring model performance • Participate in design sessions to continuously develop and improve the Cotiviti machine learning platform • Participate in other data science initiatives, collaborating with your peers to support their projects • Participate in knowledge sharing sessions to bring new insights and technologies to the team • Provide End to End value-based solutions, including data pipeline, model creation and application for end user consumption • Complete all responsibilities as outlined in the annual performance review and/or goal setting • Complete all special projects and other duties as assigned • Must be able to perform duties with or without reasonable accommodation Skills • Graduate degree in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science/Engineering, Statistics, Operations Research covering Advanced Statistics, Machine learning and AI • Experience with the latest techniques in natural language processing including transformers, fine-tuning LLMs, measuring/benchmarking and deploying LLMs with tools such as HuggingFace, Langchain, LLAMA/Mistral and OpenAI, vector databases • 5+ years of hands-on data science/AI experience, using typical machine learning and data science tools including pandas, scikit-learn, keras, nltk, and TensorFlow/PyTorch, GPU • Experience building production-grade machine learning deployments on AWS, Azure, or GCP • Experience working with Apache Spark™ and large-scale distributed datasets • Experience communicating technical concepts to non-technical and technical audiences is a plus • Flexibility to work with global teams as well geographically dispersed US based teams • Professional with ability to properly handle confidential information • Ability to work within a matrixed organization • Proficiency in all required skills and competencies above Benefits • Medical • Dental • Vision • Disability • Life insurance coverage • 401(k) savings plans • Paid family leave • 9 paid holidays per year • 17-27 days of Paid Time Off (PTO) per year, depending on specific level and length of service with Cotiviti. Company Overview • Cotiviti enables healthcare organizations to deliver better care at lower cost through advanced technology and data analytics that improve the quality and sustainability of healthcare in the United States. It was founded in 1979, and is headquartered in South Jordan, UT, US, with a workforce of 5001-10000 employees. Its website is Company H1B Sponsorship • Cotiviti has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 165 in 2025, 118 in 2024, 90 in 2023, 102 in 2022, 72 in 2021, 67 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.

This position will support computational oncology and cancer informatics research initiatives focused on transforming complex clinical data into structured, actionable datasets for research, quality improvement, clinical trial identification, and care delivery optimization. The role will emphasize applied machine learning, natural language processing, and large language model-driven workflows using real-world clinical data, including electronic health record data, pathology reports, radiology reports, clinical notes, genomics, treatment data, and other institutional data sources.

The ideal candidate will have practical prior experience developing data science workflows in Python and using modern machine learning or LLM-based tools in real projects.

The Senior Engineer, AI is responsible for building AI and Machine learning models and pipelines, with a focus on generative AI, LLMs and predictive modelling. I am a Lead Machine Learning (AI/ML) Engineer with experience in Agentic AI, Generative AI, Prompt Engineering, Deep Learning, Natural Language Processing (NLP) & Predictive Modelling across Omnichannel Analytics, Consumer Experience, Fraud Waste & Abuse, Marketing and Contact Center Operation Analytics. Competencies: Large Language Models, Prompt Engineering, Deep Learning, Predictive modelling, Descriptive analysis, Natural language processing (NLP), Computer vision, Model deployment and monitoring Tech Stack: Python, H2O, Hive, Spark, SQL, Domino, Power BI, R, SAS, Excel ^ Large language models for summarization, classification ^ Developed Chat bots and Bot containment enhancement frameworks ^ Trained custom large language models for classification tasks. ^ End to end development and deployment of deep learning classifiers trained over large conversational text data. ^ Developed & deployed predictive solutions for healthcare call center operations saving $2 Million+ annually. ^ Built solutions to segment financially distressed membership to be helped proactively. ^ Built a solution to identify key areas of consumer dissatisfaction with regards to their Net Promoter Score. ^ Asynchronous and general chat landscape and effectiveness differentiation study.

results.title: Principal Data Scientist - Generative AI, Machine Learning, Python, R - Remote | Wfh
highlights:
Principal Data Scientist - Generative AI, Machine Learning, Python, R - Remote | Wfh
...
# Principal Data Scientist - Generative AI, Machine Learning, Python, R - Remote
...
Responsible for overseeing data science projects, managing and mentoring a team, and aligning data initiatives with business goals. Lead the development and implementation of data models, collaborate with cross-functional teams, and stay updated on industry trends. Ensure ethical data use and communicate complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders. Lead initiatives on model governance and model ops to align with regulatory and security requirements. This role requires technical expertise, strategic thinking, and leadership to drive data-driven decision-making within the organization and be the pioneer on generative AI healthcare solutions, aimed at revolutionizing healthcare operations as well as enhancing member experience.
...
- Research and Development: Stay current with the latest advancements in AI and machine learning and apply these insights to improve existing models and develop new methodologies.
...
- AI Model Deployment, Monitoring & Model Governance: Deploy AI models into production environments, monitor their performance, and adjust as necessary to maintain accuracy and effectiveness and meet all governance and regulatory requirements.
...
- Innovation Projects: Lead pilot projects to test and implement new AI technologies within the organization
...
- Machine Learning Model Development: Design, develop, and train machine learning models using a variety of algorithms and techniques, including supervised and unsupervised learning, deep learning, and reinforcement learning.
...
- Agentic Workflows Implementation: Develop and implement agentic workflows that utilize AI agents for autonomous task execution, enhancing operational efficiency and decision-making capabilities.
...
- RAG Pattern Utilization: Employ retrieval-augmented generation patterns to improve the performance of language models, ensuring they can access and utilize external knowledge effectively to enhance their outputs.
...
- Collaboration: Work closely with cross-functional teams, including software engineers, product managers, and business analysts, to integrate AI solutions into existing systems and processes.
...
- Partner closely with business and other technology teams to build ML models which helps in improving Star ratings, reduce care gap and other business objectives.
...
Master's Degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, or a related field
...
- 10+ years' work experience as a data scientist preferably in healthcare environment but candidates with suitable experience in other industries will be considered
...
- Knowledge of big data technologies (e.g., Hadoop, Spark)
...
- Technical Proficiency: Strong programming skills in languages such as Python and R, and experience with machine learning frameworks like TensorFlow, Keras, or PyTorch.
...
- Statistical Analysis: Excellent understanding of statistical methods and machine learning algorithms, including k-NN, Naive Bayes, SVM, and neural networks.
...
- Experience with Agentic Workflows: Familiarity with designing and implementing agentic workflows that leverage AI agents for autonomous operations.
...
- RAG Techniques: Knowledge of retrieval-augmented generation techniques and their application in enhancing AI model outputs.
...
- Model Fine-Tuning Expertise: Proven experience in fine-tuning models for specific tasks, ensuring they meet the required performance metrics.
...
PREFERRED EDUCATION:
...
PHD or additional experience
...
- PREFERRED EXPERIENCE:
- Experience with cloud platforms (e.g., Databricks, Snowflake, Azure AI Studio etc.) for working with AI workflows and deploying models.
- Familiarity with natural language processing (NLP) and computer vision techniques.
